negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing RITUAL_ZERO_POOL_SCHEDULED payload-builder mode and transaction-hash de-duplication for merged scheduled candidates.
related_but_insufficient_code: A metrics comment references the convention for is_zero_pool_scheduled_enabled, but no payload implementation or function definition was found locally.
The payload builder de-duplicates Scheduler call_id/index with included_scheduled_indices, not merged scheduled candidate transaction hash under a zero-pool mode.

rationale: The later candidate supersedes the initial kill switch by applying it consistently to coverage and async collection and adding hash de-duplication.
statement: When RITUAL_ZERO_POOL_SCHEDULED=1, payload building must ignore pool-sourced scheduled transactions for contract-state coverage and async collection, rely on augmented scheduled candidates, and de-duplicate merged scheduled candidates by transaction hash before simulation.
suggested_falsification_tests: Set RITUAL_ZERO_POOL_SCHEDULED=1 and provide a pool-sourced scheduled tx plus the same contract-state obligation; assert pool source is ignored and merged candidates are hash de-duplicated.
Search payload logs/candidates to confirm pool-sourced scheduled txs do not affect contract-state coverage or async collection under the env flag.
summary: Zero-pool scheduled mode is absent from payload building.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Current port has no RITUAL_ZERO_POOL_SCHEDULED payload code path to exercise.
title: Zero-pool scheduled mode ignores pool candidates consistently
